name: Integration & Testing
description: Testing strategies, E2E testing, contract testing, and chaos engineering
axioms: Testing pyramid: many unit tests, some integration tests, few E2E tests
Test isolation: tests should not depend on external state or other tests
Arrange-Act-Assert: set up, execute, verify (AAA pattern)
Test doubles: mocks, stubs, fakes — choose by what you're verifying
Property-based testing: specify properties, let framework generate test cases
Shift left: test earlier in the development cycle (cheaper to fix)
reasoning_patterns: Test design: boundary values, equivalence classes, error conditions
Contract testing: verify API contracts between services (Pact)
Mutation testing: verify test quality by introducing bugs
Chaos engineering: inject failures to verify resilience (Netflix Chaos Monkey)
Test data management: factories, fixtures, realistic synthetic data
boundary_conditions: Does not cover performance testing in depth (load, stress, soak)
Visual regression testing requires specialized tools
Formal verification is a separate field
key_references: Feathers, M. Working Effectively with Legacy Code
canonical_truths: Tests are code. Maintain them like code.
100% code coverage does not mean bug-free (only tests what you wrote)
Flaky tests destroy trust in the test suite (fix or delete them)
The best tests document intent, not implementation
TDD is a design technique, not a testing technique
